name: ExtensionCitation_EffectivePeriod
title: R5: When the citation record is expected to be used (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Citation.effectivePeriod` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Citation.effectivePeriod` 0..1 `Period`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Citation.effectivePeriod` 0..1 `Period`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Citation.effectivePeriod` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `Citation` is not mapped.
Element `Citation.effectivePeriod` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: When the citation record is expected to be used (new)
element.comment:
Element `Citation.effectivePeriod` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `Citation` is not mapped.
Element `Citation.effectivePeriod` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
The effective period for a citation record determines when the content is applicable for usage and is independent of publication and review dates. For example, a citation intended to be used for the year 2016 might be published in 2015.

See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](https://hl7.org/fhir/canonicalreso.html#localization).

element.short: When the citation record is expected to be used
element.definition: The period during which the citation record content was or is planned to be in active use.
element.comment:
The effective period for a citation record determines when the content is applicable for usage and is independent of publication and review dates. For example, a citation intended to be used for the year 2016 might be published in 2015.

See guidance around (not) making local changes to elements [here](https://hl7.org/fhir/canonicalreso.html#localization).
